# Quiz Game – Copilot Instructions

1. **Read the intent first**: `docs/plan.md` (roadmap) + `docs/structure.md` (architecture) are the north star. Step guides (`docs/01-setup-project.md` through `docs/04-presentation-and-realtime.md`) describe the expected workflow per phase—update them when behavior shifts.
2. **Layered layout**: Domain logic lives in `src/domain/**`, orchestrated by `src/application/use-cases` + `src/application/services`, persisted via Prisma repositories under `src/infrastructure/**`, and exposed to React through TanStack-powered hooks in `src/hooks` / feature folders. Never let React components reach into entities directly—pass DTOs only.
3. **Presentation rules**: Next.js App Router (`src/app/**`) mixes server/client components. Server components fetch initial DTOs; client components subscribe via TanStack Query + WebSockets. Mirror the UX from `docs/mockups/*.png` (host dashboard, join screen, round results) before inventing new layouts.
4. **Data contracts**: Introduce/extend schemas in `src/application/dtos` (use zod) whenever an API route or service signature changes. DTOs are the single source for both Prisma mappers and UI props; regenerate tests if you add/rename fields.
5. **Repositories & Prisma**: Repository interfaces live in `src/domain/repositories`. Implement them in `src/infrastructure/repositories` using Prisma models defined under `src/infrastructure/database/prisma`. Keep migrations in sync with Supabase and document connection env vars in `.env.example` (create/update if missing).
6. **Services & hooks**: Application services return serializable DTOs and should stay side-effect free except for repository/websocket calls. Hooks (e.g., `useHostSession`) wrap these services plus TanStack Query cache updates—update both when adding endpoints so optimistic UI stays consistent.
7. **Testing workflow**: Vitest drives all unit/integration tests; see `src/tests/**` for existing coverage. Use `yarn test` for the suite, `yarn test:watch` during development, and prefer in-memory doubles for domain tests. When touching repositories, add contract tests that hit the Prisma test DB.
8. **Linting & formatting**: Run `yarn lint` (ESLint flat config in `eslint.config.mjs`) and `yarn lint:fix` before committing. Tailwind 4 uses `@import "tailwindcss"` in `src/app/globals.css`; use the design tokens already configured instead of inline styles.
9. **Dev & build commands**: Always run `nvm use` before any terminal command so the repo’s Node version is active. Use Yarn scripts only (`yarn dev`, `yarn build`, `yarn start`, `yarn lint`, `yarn test`). Dev server runs `next dev --turbopack`. Keep `vitest.config.ts` + `tsconfig.json` path aliases updated when moving files.
10. **UI system**: Tailwind 4 + shadcn drive all styling. Reuse primitives under `src/components/ui` or add new ones via `yarn shadcn add`; log additions in `components.json`. Avoid inline styles and keep tokens in `src/app/globals.css`.
11. **Realtime & hosting**: Default assumption is Vercel for host/admin UI plus Supabase for Postgres + Storage; Socket.IO/WebSocket gateway may move to a dedicated worker later. Use the adapter described in `docs/04-presentation-and-realtime.md` so we can switch transports without touching components.
12. **Toolbox quick refs**:
	- Prisma: `yarn prisma:migrate`, `yarn prisma:generate` (after `nvm use`).
	- Tests: `yarn test`, `yarn test:watch`, `yarn test:coverage`.
	- Lint/format: `yarn lint`, `yarn lint:fix`.
	- Docs remind us to run everything via Yarn; avoid `npx`/`npm`.
13. **Documentation cadence**: After significant architecture or workflow changes, update the matching step doc (`docs/0x-*.md`) so future agents stay in sync. Missing doc updates are considered regressions.
14. **Session target tracker**: Every working session gets its own checklist under `docs/progress/sessions/<DATE>-session-targets.md`. If multiple sessions happen on the same day, append a short focus slug (e.g., `2025-11-30-prisma-session-targets.md`) so files stay unique. Each file should open with a one-line title describing the session focus, list active goals at the top, and move finished items to the "Completed" section before starting a new file for the next block of work.
14. **Iterative workflow**: Before touching code, reread the relevant docs (plan, structure, step guides, action logs) and inspect the current files so you understand the present state. Implement in tight loops, then document what changed plus any follow-up ideas so the next agent inherits the context.
15. **Idea ledger**: Capture per-file ideas/todos in `docs/progress/file-ideas.md`. Update it whenever you defer work or discover future enhancements so context isn't lost between sessions.
16. **Ideal location of doc notes**: When adding new documentation (e.g., env var onboarding, setup steps), place it in the most relevant existing doc (e.g., `docs/01-setup-project.md` for env vars) or create a new doc under `docs/` if nothing fits. Avoid scattering notes in README or code comments unless they are extremely transient. Dev notes belongs to `docs/dev-notes.md` for short descriptions of performed actions. Particular steps in implementation like what have done belongs to `actions/**.md` files.
